content.headline: St Andrews Old Course Pint Glass
content.description_html:
The Old Course at St Andrews is golf's most storied stage. Managed by The Royal and Ancient in Fife, Scotland, it's the kind of place where every golfer wants to play at least once—and most want to play again immediately after.

This 18-hole layout plays to a par of 72 across 6,671 yards from the White tees. The course moves with the natural terrain in ways that feel both timeless and slightly mischievous. The 17th hole, the Road Hole, is the stuff of legend: a par 4 that has humbled champions and haunted their dreams for decades.

Sip from this glass and remember your round—or dream about the one you're planning. Because at St Andrews, golf isn't just a game. It's a pilgrimage.
bullets: Par 72, 6,671 yards from the White tees
18 holes with a course rating of 73.1 and slope of 132
Plan for roughly 3 hours 36 minutes per round
